an1.268:1.1: "It is impossible, *bhikkhus*, it cannot happen, that a person accomplished in right view would regard any *saṅkhāra* as permanent [*aniccā*]. That is not possible.
an1.268:1.2: But it is possible, *bhikkhus*, that an ordinary person would regard some *saṅkhāra* as permanent [*aniccā*]. That is possible."
an1.269:1.1: "It is impossible, *bhikkhus*, it cannot happen, that a person accomplished in right view would regard any *saṅkhāra* as *sukha*. That is not possible.
an1.269:1.2: But it is possible, *bhikkhus*, that an ordinary person would regard some *saṅkhāra* as *sukha*. That is possible."
an1.270:1.1: "It is impossible, *bhikkhus*, it cannot happen, that a person accomplished in right view would regard any *dhamma* as self. That is not possible.
an1.270:1.2: But it is possible, *bhikkhus*, that an ordinary person would regard some *dhamma* as self. That is possible."
an1.271:1.1: "It is impossible, *bhikkhus*, it cannot happen, that a person accomplished in right view would take the life of their mother. That is not possible.
an1.271:1.2: But it is possible, *bhikkhus*, that an ordinary person would take the life of their mother. That is possible."
an1.272:1.1: "It is impossible, *bhikkhus*, it cannot happen, that a person accomplished in right view would take the life of their father. That is not possible.
an1.272:1.2: But it is possible, *bhikkhus*, that an ordinary person would take the life of their father. That is possible."
an1.273:1.1: "It is impossible, *bhikkhus*, it cannot happen, that a person accomplished in right view would take the life of an *arahant*. That is not possible.
an1.273:1.2: But it is possible, *bhikkhus*, that an ordinary person would take the life of an *arahant*. That is possible."
an1.274:1.1: "It is impossible, *bhikkhus*, it cannot happen, that a person accomplished in right view would, with a mind [*citta*] of malice, draw blood from the Tathāgata. That is not possible.
an1.274:1.2: But it is possible, *bhikkhus*, that an ordinary person would, with a mind [*citta*] of malice, draw blood from the Tathāgata. That is possible."
an1.275:1.1: "It is impossible, *bhikkhus*, it cannot happen, that a person accomplished in right view would cause a schism in the *Saṅgha*. That is not possible.
an1.275:1.2: But it is possible, *bhikkhus*, that an ordinary person would cause a schism in the *Saṅgha*. That is possible."
an1.276:1.1: "It is impossible, *bhikkhus*, it cannot happen, that a person accomplished in right view would take refuge in another teacher. That is not possible.
an1.276:1.2: But it is possible, *bhikkhus*, that an ordinary person would take refuge in another teacher. That is possible."
an1.277:1.1: "It is impossible, *bhikkhus*, it cannot happen, that two *arahants* who are fully awakened would arise simultaneously in one world-system. That is not possible.
an1.277:1.2: But it is possible, *bhikkhus*, that one *arahant* who is fully awakened would arise in one world-system. That is possible."
